info.title: Microsoft.TemplateEngine.Authoring.CLI
info.description: A dotnet CLI tool with commands for template authoring.

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: Command.Parse, OK: Command.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: ParseResult.Invoke, OK: ParseErrorAction.Invoke, OK: HelpAction.Invoke, OK: ParseResult.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

commands.name: export
commands.description: Given a template.config file, creates a "localize" directory next to it and exports the localization files into the created directory. If the localization files already exist, the existing translations will be preserved.

options.name: recursive
options.description: When specified, subdirectories are also searched for template.json files. [default: False]

options.name: --language
options.description: The list of languages to be supported by this template. The following language list will be used as default if this option is omitted: cs, de, en, es, fr, it, ja, ko, pl, pt-BR, ru, tr, zh-Hans, zh-Hant

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: If this option is specified, modified files will not be saved to file system and the changes will only be printed to console output. [default: False]

arguments.name: template-path
arguments.description: Specifies the path to the template.json file. If a directory is given, template.json file will be searched within the directory. If --recursive options is specified, all the template.json files under the given directory and its subdirectories will be taken as input.

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Runs the template with specified arguments and compares the result with expectations files.

options.name: --template-args
options.description: Template specific arguments - all joined into single enquoted string. Any needed quotations of actual arguments has to be escaped.

options.name: --template-path
options.description: Specifies the path to the directory with template to be verified.

options.name: --output
options.description: Specifies the path to target directory to output the generated template to.

options.name: --snapshots-directory
options.description: Specifies path to the directory with snapshot files.

options.name: --scenario-name
options.description: Specifies optional scenario name to be used in the snapshot folder name.

options.name: --disable-diff-tool
options.description: If set to true - the diff tool won't be automatically started by the Verifier on verification failures. [default: False]

options.name: --disable-default-exclude-patterns
options.description: If set to true - all template output files will be verified, unless --exclude-pattern option is used. [default: False]

options.name: --exclude-pattern
options.description: Specifies pattern(s) defining files to be excluded from verification.

options.name: --include-pattern
options.description: Specifies pattern(s) defining files to be included to verification (all files are included if not specified).

options.name: --verify-std
options.description: If set to true - 'dotnet new' command standard output and error contents will be verified along with the produced template files. [default: False]

options.name: --fail-expected
options.description: If set to true - 'dotnet new' command is expected to return non-zero return code. [default: False]

options.name: --unique-for
options.description: Sets the Verifier expectations directory naming convention, by indicating which scenarios should be differentiated.

arguments.name: template-short-name
arguments.description: Name of the template to be verified. Can be already installed template or a template within local path specified with -p/--template-path option.

commands.name: validate
commands.description: The location of template(s) to validate.

arguments.name: template-location
arguments.description: Validates the templates at given location.
